---
title: "What you need — Exercise Sheet"
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Official Documentation
See **[quarto.org/docs/output-formats/pdf-basics.html](https://quarto.org/docs/output-formats/pdf-basics.html)** for more about PDF output options.
:::

To create an exercise sheet you need the same setup as for a website, plus one extra tool if you want PDF output.

---

## Step 1 — VS Code + Quarto extension

If you have not done this yet, follow [What you need — Website](beg_website_1.qmd) and come back here when done.

---

## Step 2 — Install TinyTeX (for PDF output only)

Quarto needs a LaTeX engine to create PDFs. The easiest option is **TinyTeX**, which Quarto can install for you directly from VS Code.

Open the **Terminal** (the command line interface) in VS Code via **View → Terminal**, and run:

```{.bash filename="Terminal"}
quarto install tinytex
```

Wait for the installation to finish — this can take a few minutes.

::: {.callout-note}
If you only want to produce **HTML** or **Word** output, you can skip this step entirely.
:::

---

::: {.callout-tip}
## ✅ Ready!
Continue with [Create an Exercise Sheet →](beg_exercise_2.qmd)
:::
